stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core areas of transformers and large language models (LLMs).
The context is related to text generation capabilities within the AI ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML domains like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of Meta's Llama architecture indicates continued high interest in leading open-source LLMs.

stories.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA has selected Relativity Space, a company associated with Eric Schmidt, for a 2028 Mars mission.
Relativity Space will handle the entire mission scope, including the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations.
The mission involves launching NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars under a new public-private partnership.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft discovered a new, lightweight backdoor malware named Crypto Clipper.
The malware is designed to steal cryptocurrency from infected systems.
It spreads via USB drives and utilizes the Tor network for communication.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: Identifies that fine-tuning LLMs on insecure code causes emergent misalignment due to poor internal structure understanding.
Proposes and tests an 'actionable activation-space direction' to detect this misalignment across multiple model families (Qwen2.5, Gemma-2, Llama-3.2, Mistral-3).
Achieves extremely high separation (99.6%) of aligned vs. misaligned activations using a difference-in-means direction at the final layer.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function as a generative AI.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in its MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing LLM execution on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents that run entirely on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously voted to approve Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of administrative difficulty, including a refusal by a Trump official to review the vaccine in February.
This signifies a major regulatory milestone for mRNA technology in the vaccine market.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for defense purposes.
This increased spending is linked to geopolitical tensions involving China.
The defense spending is also expected to have positive economic impacts overseas.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
Significant changes related to this verification system are scheduled to begin in September.
The update will define timelines and specify which app stores are supported under the new framework.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses a key limitation in state-of-the-art flow-matching TTS: the inability to adapt to new pronunciations post-deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that learns pronunciation corrections via latent conditioning edits instead of full model retraining.
The method optimizes token-level perturbations in the text embedding space when corrective feedback is provided.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces StylisticBias, a new controlled benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in MLLM research: separating appearance-based biases from genuine identity differences in visual judgments.
Highlights the growing societal risk associated with poorly understood visual cues shaping consequential AI deployments.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper proposes a novel method to align Large Language Models (LLMs) using implicit user feedback rather than relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Existing LLM alignment methods are limited by the high cost and scarcity of explicit human preference annotations.
The research aims to leverage implicit signals, similar to those used by major internet platforms, to improve LLM alignment efficiency.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local use on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Philips Hue introduced wired wall modules to integrate non-smart lights into its ecosystem.
The new modules are designed to be installed behind existing wall switches.
Hue also released more affordable versions of its Signe series lamps and updated E14 bulbs.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK government plans to use facial recognition technology to verify the age of asylum seekers.
Independent tests have demonstrated that the age-verification technology carries a significant risk of making life-altering errors.
The deployment proceeds despite known flaws in the accuracy and reliability of the system.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of understanding regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
A novel method, cross-attention attribution, is proposed and adapted from the DAAM framework to analyze speech diffusion models.
The technique generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers to improve the controllability and diagnose failure modes in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where state (facts, constraints) must be reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on improving policy-adherence in tool-calling agents, particularly for complex domains like customer service.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: DeepSWIP addresses the limitation of associational inference in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
It introduces a single-world counterfactual semantics to enable causal reasoning for interventions and evidence.
The method utilizes neural materialization to map fixed-context neural predicates to standard ProbLog choices for counterfactual computation.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
The dataset addresses limitations in existing SAR-optical resources, which often lack complex-valued measurements and native acquisition geometry.
This resource is crucial for advancing physically grounded multimodal learning in foundation models, particularly for very-high-resolution (VHR) applications.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of the LiveCodeBench (LCB) benchmark.
LCB is a recognized benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, known for its contamination-aware design.
Multi-LCB addresses LCB's limitation by extending the evaluation framework to support multiple programming languages beyond Python.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM that operates significantly in a continuous latent space.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and another component (implied).

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a fine-grained alternative to traditional Differential Privacy (DP).
Addresses the costly worst-case privacy-accuracy tradeoff inherent in standard DP.
Explicitly models privacy leakage by incorporating attacker knowledge, compromised data portions, and query families.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, unlike traditional high-throughput systems.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' to manage the full execution state, not just the standard KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and rapid state restoration.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of LLM coding agents: their lack of high-level operational knowledge about a software repository.
The paper investigates the effectiveness of using contextual guidance (like AGENTS.md files) to improve agent performance.
It proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning' method to better guide coding agents within a repository context.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured state representation.
Addresses a key limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly managing state across tool calls and policy adherence.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale, bilingual (German/English) dataset of 1.2M CT/MR image-text pairs for radiology.
Develops RefRad2D to enable training of spatially grounded Vision-Language Models (VLMs) without manual spatial annotations.
The resulting model, RadGrounder, performs joint tasks including report generation and visual question answering in a clinical setting.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Introduces 'CATCH-ME if you RAG,' a new dataset specifically designed for multi-turn counterspeech generation.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating high-quality, non-repetitive responses against harmful online exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical need for optimizing large model inference to ensure scalable and low-cost operation.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture for token-oriented inference optimization.
The architecture comprises Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a framework designed to integrate automated essay scoring (AES) with psychometric principles.
Addresses limitations in current AES by coupling scoring with learner proficiency to provide adaptive feedback.
Aims to bridge the gap between opaque neural scoring models and non-contextual LLM-based feedback.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to differentiate true communicative intent from surface-level sentiment.
Highlights that AI failure in Nigerian discourse is primarily due to context failure, not translation failure, challenging existing sentiment benchmarks.

stories.title: GPT-5.5 hallucinates 3x more than MIT-licensed GLM-5.2
stories.author: oshrimpton
bullet_points: Compares the hallucination rates of two large language models: GPT-5.5 and GLM-5.2.
GPT-5.5 reportedly exhibits a significantly higher rate of hallucination (3x more) compared to GLM-5.2.
The comparison highlights ongoing challenges in AI model reliability and accuracy across different architectures.

stories.title: Nobody Knows How Many AI Agents They're Running
stories.author: ntctech
bullet_points: The article discusses the lack of visibility into the actual number of AI agents currently deployed.
It draws a parallel between tracking AI agents and the established practice of counting virtual machines (VMs) in infrastructure.
This suggests a growing operational challenge in managing the proliferation of autonomous AI systems.

stories.title: How I built on-device AI background removal in a native Windows app
stories.author: iammxfschr
bullet_points: The article details the development of an on-device AI background removal feature.
It addresses a privacy concern by avoiding server uploads, unlike many existing tools.
The implementation was done within a native Windows application.

stories.title: RAG Pipeline: Complete Node.js Implementation Guide
stories.author: surajrkhonde
bullet_points: Provides a complete Node.js implementation guide for building Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems.
Focuses on practical aspects, detailing where RAG pipelines can fail and how they function.
Aimed at developers looking to deploy production-ready RAG solutions using Node.js.

stories.title: RAG Pipeline: The Uncle-Nephew Complete Learning Guide
stories.author: surajrkhonde
bullet_points: The article focuses on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines.
It addresses the critical problem of Large Language Models (LLMs) hallucinating.
It serves as a practical, comprehensive guide for implementing data-aware AI systems.
